Transfer hazelnuts to a clean tea towel. Bundle up and rub vigorously with hands to remove most of the skin (which is bitter). (No need to do this with almonds)
Roughly chop hazelnuts then add into a jug just large enough to fit the head of a stick blender with remaining ingredients. Blitz until it's like pesto – not completely smooth. Use per recipe.

title: Tom Yum Soup (Thai Soup)
description: Tom Yum soup – everybody's favourite Thai soup is finally here! Choose from the classic clear Tom Yum Goong, or make the creamy Tom Yum version – both are made with the same broth. Simple to make using whole fresh OR peeled frozen prawns / shrimp, this is astonishingly easy to make once you've gathered the ingredients! Bonus: Tom Yum is probably the healthiest Thai dish ever, clocking in at just 156 calories!
author_notes: 1. Lemongrass – To prepare, cut in half, peel the reedy green shell to reveal the softer white part on the bottom half of the lemongrass. If lemongrass is hard to come by, you can use PASTE: stir in 1 tbsp. 2. Galangal is like ginger but it has a more sour and peppery flavour. If you can't find it, just substitute ginger and a grind of black pepper. 3. Kaffir Lime Leaves are the leaves of a kaffir lime tree. It's used to add earthy citrus flavours into Asian food. Sold at large grocery stores (Coles, Woolies, Harris) and Asian stores. Dried is an ok substitute (same amount), but I really urge you to try to find fresh if you can because it adds that "something-something" that really makes this "restaurant quality". Freezes 100% perfectly, I always have a stash! 4. Chilli – This soup is not that spicy but has a mild hum to it! 5. Fish Sauce – I'm afraid this is key to getting enough flavour in the broth so I can't offer a sub here! 6. Thai roasted Chilli Paste – you'll need to go to a Thai grocery store for this. If you can't get to one, use another Asian Chilli Paste and some chilli oil to achieve a similar flavour and effect. Sambal Oelak + chilli oil is an excellent fall back. 7. SHORTCUT FROZEN PRAWN/SHRIMP VERSION: I wanted to make a faster version made using pre-peeled frozen prawns. Even I was astonished how well it came out! Here's how to make it: Skip prawn/shrimp shells and head in the broth. Instead, add 1.5 tsp shrimped paste into the broth. Proceed with recipe. Use 200g/7oz frozen peeled prawns (this is more than the weight of peeled whole prawns) and add them in step 7. The resulting soup is extremely good, quite similar to making the broth using the prawn shells! 8. Storing – leftover soup can be kept for 2 days in the fridge then gently reheat in microwave without prawns/shrimp and add them in later (so you don't accidentally overcook them). Freezing – broth freezes 100% perfectly.

ingredients.item: whole prawns / shrimp with heads and shells on
ingredients.original_text: 300g / 10oz whole prawns / shrimp with heads and shells on (Note 7)
1/2 cup (125 ml) chicken stock/broth , low sodium
2 stalks of lemongrass , outer layers peeled(Note 1)
1.5 cm / 3/5" piece of galangal , cut into 4 slices (Note 2)
1/2 white onion (medium sized), cut into wedges about 1 cm thick
1 1/2 tbsp Thai roasted chili sauce (Nam Prik Pa, Note 6)

steps.instruction: Peel the prawns. Place heads and shell in pot, reserve meat.
Use a meat mallet or similar to bash the garlic, chilli and lemongrass so they burst open to release flavour. Add into pot.
Crush kaffir lime leaves with your hands. Add into pot.
Add galangal, stock and water. Bring to simmer on high heat, cover, then reduce to medium and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the broth, discard the prawn shells etc, then return broth into same pot over low heat.
Add onions and mushrooms, simmer 3 minutes. Add tomatoes, simmer for 1 minute.
Add prawns, simmer 2 minutes or until just cooked.
Stir in sugar and fish sauce, simmer for 1 minute.
Add lime juice, then taste. Adjust sweet (sugar), salt (fish sauce) and sour (lime) to your taste (trust me, you'll know when you like it!).
Ladle into bowls and serve with fresh coriander and fresh chilli! This is the CLEAR version of Tom Yum.
When you add sugar, also add Thai Chilli Paste and evaporated milk.
Then continue with recipe!
Following Creamy Tom Yum steps but use coconut milk instead of evaporated milk.

title: Vegetable Beef Soup (Fall apart beef!)
description: A hearty Vegetable Beef Soup with melt-in-your-mouth slow cooked chunks of beef, potato and vegetables simmered in a herb infused savoury broth. You'll love how the broth of this beef soup recipe is slightly thickened so it's like gravy and has extra flavour from a secret ingredient!
author_notes: 1. Beef – Use any stewing or braising beef, usually sold pre cut into small pieces ideal for this recipe. Otherwise, get a slow cooking cut like chuck or boneless short rib, then cut the beef into pieces. Look for beef that's nicely marbled with fat for best results! 2. Red Wine, Guinness or Stout – will add incredible extra flavour into this soup broth! I use all 3, and enjoy them all. If you love Irish Beef Guinness Stew, you'll know what the Guinness does to add flavour. Red wine – any dry, full bodied red wine. Cab Sauv and Merlots are ideal. Guinness Beer and Stout are dark beers, labelled as such on the cans. You should find these at most liquor stores. Use some, drink some – or tip the whole can in an simmer for longer with the lid off! Non alcoholic sub – add 400g/14 oz can of crushed tomato, 2 tsp Worcestershire sauce. This will add alternative flavour to compensate. 3. Storage – Like stew, it's even better the next day! Will keep for 4 to 5 days in the fridge. Also freezes perfectly. 4. Nutrition per serving.

ingredients.original_text: 500g/1 lb stewing beef , cut into 1.75cm / 2/3″ cubes (Note 1)
3 carrots , cut into 0.5 cm / 1/5″ thick slices (halve larger ones)

ingredients.item: dry red wine, guinness beer or stout
ingredients.original_text: 1 1/2 cups (375ml) dry red wine, Guinness beer or stout (Note 2)

steps.instruction: Heat 1 tbsp oil until very hot in a large, heavy based pot over high heat.
Pat beef dry with paper towels, then sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Brown beef aggressively in 2 or 3 batches, adding more oil if needed. Remove browned beef into a bowl.
If pot looks dry, add a touch more oil.
Add garlic and onion, cook for 2 minutes.
Add carrot and celery, cook for 2 minutes or until onion is translucent.
Stir in flour, then slowly pour in beef broth while constantly stirring.
Add beer, water, tomato paste, bay leaves and thyme, stir well. Then add the beef back in.
Cover, adjust heat to medium low so it's bubbling gently. Simmer 1 hr 15 min or until beef is pretty tender.
Add potatoes and peas, simmer for a further 20 minutes without the lid. Add cooked mushrooms in the last 5 minutes.
The soup is ready when the potatoes are cooked and beef is very tender (see video).
Adjust salt and pepper to taste (I like lots of pepper in this!).
Ladle into bowls. Sprinkle with parsley and serve with crusty bread if desired. Try quick Cheesy Garlic Bread or super easy Irish Soda Bread!
Melt butter in a large skillet over medium high heat. Add mushrooms and cook for 5 minutes until browned. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.

title: Chorizo chickpea stew (quick!)
description: This chickpea stew owes its speed and deliciousness to chorizo. It injects a stack of flavour into the thick sauce, and biting into glorious chunks of golden brown chorizo will probably be the best thing that happens to you today. Despite the speed, you will not feel robbed of flavour by any stretch of the imagination thanks to chorizo, the powerful little flavour packed Spanish sausages that are the hero of today's dish. We sauté chorizo slices until golden brown before braising in a tomato-based stew sauce for a mere 15 minutes to infuse the sauce with chorizo flavour. Chickpeas add bulk and texture to the dish, and they too absorb the chorizo flavour!
author_notes: Chorizo are Spanish cured sausages readily found in everyday grocery stores, deli or fridge section. Usually around 125g/4oz each so you'll need 2. Alternatives: Other smoked sausages will work a treat here. Leftovers will keep for 3 to 4 days in the fridge, or 3 months in the freezer. Nutrition assumes 3 servings.

ingredients.original_text: 250g / 8 oz chorizo (2 pieces) , cut into 8mm / 1/3″ slices (Note 1)
1 bay leaf , preferably fresh, otherwise dried
400g/14 oz can chickpeas , drained (or other beans of choice)

steps.instruction: Heat olive oil in a large saucepan or small pot over medium high heat. Add chorizo and cook for 3 minutes or until golden.
Add garlic, thyme, paprika and bay leaf, then stir for 1 minute.
Add tomato, chickpeas, chicken stock, salt and pepper. Stir, bring to a simmer then lower the heat down to medium low so it is simmering very gently.
Cook for 15 minutes (no lid), stirring regularly to ensure the base does not catch, until the sauce has reduced and thickened.
Discard bay leaf and serve. I love this with hot crusty bread (slathered with butter!).

title: Jambalaya recipe
description: This proper Jambalaya recipe is for one of New Orleans' most iconic and beloved dishes! Here, a rubble of juicy plump shrimp/prawns, seared smoky sausage and tender chicken are dotted through fragrant, Creole-spiced tomato rice and tender vegetables. This easy Jambalaya is packed with big punchy Louisiana flavours and is arguably the world's best one-pot meal!
author_notes: TOP TIP: BAKE IT! You'll make your Jambalaya life a whole lot less stressful by baking the rice rather than cooking it on the stove. Jambalaya cooked on the stove requires experience. You need to master the skill of knowing how many times you can stir the rice so it cooks evenly and stop the base from burning, but ensuring you don't stir too often which turns the rice into a gluey mushy mess. Skip the mushy rice and head straight for stress-free Jambalaya nirvana by simply popping it in the oven instead. 100% hands free, Jambalaya perfection, every single time!

ingredients.original_text: 200g (7oz) andouille or smoked sausage , sliced 0.5cm / 1/5″ thick (Note 1)
300g (10oz) chicken thigh , skinless boneless, cut into 2.5cm /1″ pieces
12 prawns/shrimp , raw, large, (peeled, with or without tails)
1 onion , large, cut into 1.5 cm / 0.5″ pieces
2 celery ribs , cut into 1.5 cm / 0.5″ pieces
2 green capsicum / bell pepper , medium, cut into 1.5 cm / 0.5″ pieces
1 cup green onions , sliced, plus more for serving
2 tsp chopped fresh thyme (or 1 tsp dried thyme)
1/2 tsp cayenne powder (adjust spice to taste)

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 180C/350F (all types).
Heat oil in a very large skillet or dutch oven over medium high heat. (Note 4)
Add bacon, cook for 30 seconds (to start fat melting), then add sausages. Cook until sausages are golden – about 3 minutes – then remove into bowl.
Add chicken into the pan and cook until golden (doesn't need to cook inside), then add to bowl with bacon.
Sear prawns in the pan for 1.5 minutes on each side, then transfer to a separate bowl (reserve until later).
Add butter, then garlic, onion, celery and capsicum. Cook for 3 to 4 minutes or until slightly softened.
Add rice, stir to coat grains in oil.
Add chicken broth, tomato paste, canned tomato, thyme and Seasoning Mix.
Stir well, then add chicken, sausages and bacon (including all liquid).
When you see bubbles across most of the surface, stir well once more. Ensure all rice is submerged, cover with lid, and transfer to oven. (See video)

Bake 20 minutes. Remove lid and check rice by eating a few grains (careful, very hot!) If the rice is just about done (Note 5 for the correct consistency), go to next step. If rice grains are still firm in the centre, continue to cook, checking rice every 5 mins – most ovens take about 30 mins total cooking (see Note 7).
Add prawns/shrimp and green onions, QUICKLY (but gently!) stir through, cover with lid, and return to oven for just 3 minutes (just to heat prawns).
Remove from oven (see video for finished consistency), stir gently to fluff, then serve, garnished with more green onions if desired.

title: Birria Tacos
description: Birria Tacos – or Quesabirria – are the legendary crispy red tacos stuffed with cheesy shredded beef, served with a rich consommé for dunking. This was a beast of a recipe to crack, so I'm thrilled we got there. I'm obsessed – big flavour delivered with every single bite!
author_notes: We toss the shredded beef in sauce before stuffing for extra flavour. We give you chilli measures in weight and cups so your sauce has enough flavour. Our birrias are crispier because of the way we cook them. Our consommé is not thin and watery – it clings better to the Birria Tacos which means more flavour in every bite!

ingredients.original_text: ▢ 25 g (0.9 oz) guajillo chillies, dried (~4 pieces, heaped 3/4 cup once chopped) (Note 1)
▢ 45 g (1.6 oz) ancho chillies, dried (~2-3 pieces, 1 cup once chopped) (Note 1)
▢ 6 g (0.2 oz) chillies de arbol, dried (~6 pieces, 2 tbsp once chopped) (Note 1)
▢ 1/2 cup chilli soaking water (reserve after simmering)
▢ 1 small onion , roughly sliced (~tennis ball size)
▢ 1.3 kg/2.6 lb chuck beef , cut into 6 large pieces (or boneless beef short ribs – 1.8kg/3.6 lb bone-in) (Note 3)
▢ 2 cups beef stock / broth , low sodium
▢ 1 cinnamon stick (substitute 1/4 tsp powder, but it's not quite the same)
▢ 3 bay leaves , preferably fresh else dried
▢ 2 tbsp apple cider vinegar (or regular white vinegar, red or white wine vinegar)
▢ 2 1/2 tsp cooking salt / kosher salt (halve for table salt, +30% for flakes)
▢ 1/2 cup finely chopped coriander / cilantro leaves
▢ 3 1/2 cups (350g) shredded Colby, Monterey Jack or Oaxaca cheese (if you can get it) (Note 6)
▢ Your favorite salsa or hot sauce , optional

steps.instruction: Use gloves if you're sensitive to working with chilli!
Deseed – Trim and discard stems. Cut lengthwise, remove and discard the seeds. Roughly chop into 1 – 1.5cm / 1/4" pieces.
Simmer – Put chillis in a large saucepan of boiling water. Simmer rapidly for 10 minutes to soften, until flesh is mushy (skin stays intact but softens).
Reserve 1/2 cup of chilli-simmering water. Drain chilli in a sieve, lightly press out residual liquid.

Birria Adobo (paste) – Place cooked chillies, reserved 1/2 cup chilli water and remaining Adobo ingredients in a tall jug large enough to fit the head of a stick blender. Blitz for 20 seconds until as smooth as possible (it's not completely smooth, see video). You can also do this in a small food processor, NutriBullet or blender (Note 7).

Sear beef – Heat the oil in a large heavy based pot over high heat (I use my 24cm / 9.6" dutch oven). Add half the beef and sear each side aggressively until it's dark golden brown (about 1 – 1 1/2 minutes each side). Remove to a bowl and repeat with remaining beef.
Cook off Adobo – Add the paste to same pan and stir for 2 minutes in oil (lower heat if spitting aggressively).

Slow cook 2 1/2 hours – Add all Birria braising ingredients, stir, then add the beef. Bring to a boil then lower heat until it's simmering very gently (small gentle bubbles every now and then). Cover with a lid and cook for 2 1/2 hours, or until beef shreds easily (short ribs take 3 – 3.5 hrs). (Note 8 for slow cooker)
Shred – Remove the beef to a clean pan and shred well. Add the 1/2 teaspoon of salt and toss meat to distribute.
Birria oil – Skim off as much of the red oil floating on braising liquid surface as possible (ideally 6 tbsp+) and place in a separate bowl (this is the "Birria Oil"). Precious stuff!

Soak beef – Pour 1 cup of the braising liquid ("Birria Consommé") into a large non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Let it simmer for 15 seconds, then add all the beef and toss in the sauce until it's all absorbed by the beef, there should be almost none pooled in the pan. Move beef back into the shredding pan, scraping the non-stick pan clean.

Birria Consommé – Taste and check for salt, add more if needed. It should be on the slightly salty side (because it's used for dipping) but not too salty to drink a small cup like soup. Keep warm to use for dipping once tacos are done.

Red tortillas – Heat 1 teaspoon Birria Oil in the same non-stick pan over medium-low heat. Put a tortilla in and "wipe" around to make the underside red. Once the tortilla is soft (no need to flip) – about 10 seconds – remove to a work surface red side down. Repeat twice more, heating 1/2 teaspoon Birria Oil for each tortilla.
Stuff – Spread cheese on half of each tortilla, top with beef, then onion and coriander/cilantro.
Crisp! Fold over then pan-fry 3 Birria Tacos at a time (in the same pan) over medium-high heat for 2 minutes on each side, until golden-red and crispy.
Serve – Remove and serve while hot with the Birria Consommé for dunking! (Drink leftover consommé, it might be the best soup broth you have this year).

title: Crispy pork belly banh mi
description: Here's my recipe for Crispy Pork Belly Banh Mi, possibly the best sandwich I've ever had in my life! Stuffed with juicy pork belly meat with ultra crispy crackling, this Vietnamese sandwich has all the essential banh mi fixings plus a special secret banh mi sauce. Out of all the types of banh mi I make, this is my favourite!
author_notes: The recipe for the crispy skin pork belly is based on the much-loved Crispy Pork Belly recipe. So you can have confidence that your pork WILL have perfect crispy crackling! Pork belly keeps and reheats well, and the crackling stays crispy. If not using cooked pork immediately, loosely cover with foil and it will be good for an hour to use for the banh mi. If intentionally making ahead, separate the crackling from the meat and reheat the cracking in the oven (crispy!) and meat in the microwave (retains moisture).

ingredients.category: Crispy pork belly for banh mi
ingredients.original_text: 1kg / 2 lb pork belly with skin on , NOT SCORED. Look for 3 – 3.5cm / 1.2 – 1.6" thick, even thickness with flat, unwrinkled skin, preferably not vac-packed (Note 1)

ingredients.category: Crispy pork belly for banh mi
ingredients.original_text: 1 1/2 tsp canola or vegetable oil , divided

ingredients.category: Crispy pork belly for banh mi
ingredients.original_text: 1/2 tsp Chinese Five Spice , optional (you won't miss it)

ingredients.category: Crispy pork belly for banh mi
Crispy pork belly for banh mi
Special banh mi sauce (Ca Com copy!)
Special banh mi sauce (Ca Com copy!)
Special banh mi sauce (Ca Com copy!)
ingredients.original_text: 1/2 tsp dark soy sauce (mainly for colour, sub light or regular soy)

ingredients.category: To make the banh mi (5 rolls)
ingredients.original_text: 5 crusty white rolls (lightly oven toast to crisp if crust is softened)

ingredients.category: To make the banh mi (5 rolls)
To make the banh mi (5 rolls)
To make the banh mi (5 rolls)
ingredients.original_text: 1 batch Vietnamese pickled carrots and daikon

ingredients.category: To make the banh mi (5 rolls)
ingredients.original_text: 3 green onion stems , cut to length of rolls (2 to 3 pieces per roll)

ingredients.category: To make the banh mi (5 rolls)
ingredients.original_text: 2 cucumbers , finely sliced using vegetable peeled or knife

ingredients.category: To make the banh mi (5 rolls)
ingredients.original_text: 2 cups coriander/cilantro leaves and small sprigs

ingredients.category: To make the banh mi (5 rolls)
ingredients.original_text: 3 Birds Eye or Thai chillis , finely sliced

ingredients.category: To make the banh mi (5 rolls)
ingredients.original_text: 8 tbsp crispy fried shallots , store bought (Note 3)

steps.instruction: Place pork belly on a plate. Pat skin dry with paper towels. Leave uncovered in fridge overnight to dry out the skin. (If you don't have time, pat the skin dry as best you can).
steps.category: Crispy pork belly for banh mi

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 140°C/285°F (both fan and standard ovens).
steps.category: Crispy pork belly for banh mi

steps.instruction: Drizzle flesh side with 1 tsp oil. Sprinkle over 1/2 tsp salt, and all the pepper and Chinese five spice. Rub all over the flesh, including on the sides.
steps.category: Crispy pork belly for banh mi

steps.instruction: Place 2 pieces of foil on a work surface. Put the belly in middle of foil, skin side up. Fold the sides in to enclose the belly, forming an open box, pinching corners to seal tightly and make it as snug as possible. Place meat on a tray.
steps.category: Crispy pork belly for banh mi

steps.instruction: Pat skin dry with paper towels. Rub with 1/2 tsp oil then sprinkle remaining 1/2 tsp salt evenly all over the skin surface, from edge to edge.
steps.category: Crispy pork belly for banh mi

steps.instruction: Place in the oven and roast for 2 hours, tightening the foil around the pork after 1 hour (the pork will shrink). This protects the flesh from drying out.
steps.category: Crispy pork belly for banh mi

steps.instruction: Remove pork from oven, then increase heat to 240°C/465°F (all oven types).
steps.category: Crispy pork belly for banh mi

steps.instruction: Remove pork from foil, discard fat and foil. Place pork on a rack set over a tray. Use scrunched up balls of foil stuffed under pork to prop it up so the skin surface is as level and horizontal as possible (see photos in post).
steps.category: Crispy pork belly for banh mi

steps.instruction: Place the pork back in the oven for 30 minutes, rotating after 15 minutes as needed, until the crackling is deep golden, puffy and crispy all over. If some patches are browning faster, protect with foil patches.
steps.category: Crispy pork belly for banh mi

steps.instruction: Remove pork from oven. Rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
steps.category: Crispy pork belly for banh mi

steps.instruction: Mix the ingredients in a bowl then set aside. This will keep for 5 days in the fridge.
Cut the pork into 1 1/2 cm / 0.6" thick slices, then each slide into 1 1/2 cm / 0.6" chunks.
Split the roll in half down the side, keeping the lid attached. Smear the base with 1 1/2 tbsp of pate, then 1 1/2 tbsp of mayonnaise (on top of the pate)

Pile about 1/3 cup pickled vegetables on the mayonnaise. Lay 2 slices of cucumber and 2 green onions against the lid. Pile pork on top. Drizzle pork with 1 tbsp of sauce. Stuff coriander/cilantro down the side, sprinkle with chilli then2 tablespoons of crispy shallots as you want (if using).
Eat immediately! Banh mi is definitely one of those foods best eaten freshly made!

title: Orecchiette Sausage Pasta in Creamy Tomato Sauce
description: Orecchiette has one big thing going for it that other pastas do not: its cup shape makes it the perfect vehicle to hold special pasta sauces. The creamy tomato sausage pasta sauce featured in this recipe is orecchiette worthy. Decadent? Yes. Have salad tomorrow!
author_notes: KEY TIP: Avoid the cheap sausages that are a uniform pink colour – they are loaded with fillers. Look for sausages with specks of meat and white fat, as pictured above. Sausage – the better the sausage, the better the pasta! Avoid the very cheap sausages used for backyard BBQ's that are a uniform pink colour (pink = fillers = no good for cooking like this). Look for sausages where you can see chunks of meat and fat. If using link sausages (in Australia, we only get Italian sausage meat in sausage form), squeeze the meat from the casings (it's easy, demo in video). Tomato passata – pureed, strained pure tomatoes, sometimes labelled Tomato Puree in the US. Readily available in Australian supermarkets nowadays, alongside pasta sauces. If you can't find it, use crushed canned tomatoes and simmer rapidly for 5 minutes to reduce it more. Italian Herbs – just use a store bought pre mix. Otherwise, make up your own with equal amounts of thyme, basil, oregano, parsley and a pinch of rosemary. Pasta cooking water – the starch in the water thickens the sauce when tossed with pasta. So if the sauce needs thinning, best to use the pasta cooking water instead of just normal water. Great traditional Italian pasta cooking technique to use in every pasta recipe! Nutrition per serving.

ingredients.original_text: 500g / 1lb Italian pork sausages , meat squeezed out of their casings (see video, Note 1)
1/4 cup white wine , anything not too sweet (sub water)
1 cup tomato passata (called "tomato puree" in US, Note 2)
3 cups (packed) kale leaves , torn into bite size pieces (or baby spinach, swiss chard, Tuscan kale etc)

steps.instruction: Bring a large pot of water to the boil and cook pasta according to packet directions MINUS 1 minute. Just before draining, scoop out 1 cup of pasta cooking water and set aside. (Note 4)
Heat oil in a very large skillet over high heat. Add garlic and cook for 15 seconds.
Add sausage meat and cook, breaking it up as you go, until there are some little golden bits – about 4 minutes.
Add wine and cook, scraping the bottom of the skillet to remove the golden bits, and allow wine to evaporate – the winey smell will disappear.
Add remaining ingredients except kale. Stir well, bring to simmer then turn stove down to low and simmer gently for 2 minutes to bring all the flavours together.
Add pasta and kale. Toss well for 1 minute to coat pasta well with the sauce – it will thicken. If the sauce gets too thick, use the reserved pasta cooking water to loosen it up (Note 4).
Serve immediately, garnished with freshly grated parmesan

title: Homemade Chicken Noodle Soup (from scratch!)
description: A homemade Chicken Noodle Soup recipe made from scratch using a whole chicken. With a beautifully flavoured golden chicken broth, this is a classic everyone should know! Made the old fashioned way by boiling a whole chicken, you can toss in any vegetables, noodles or pasta and it's going to be amazing. And everybody knowns Chicken Soup is the best soup for a cold!
author_notes: SCALING note: If you use the servings slider to adjust for chicken size, IGNORE the change to the water required for simmering. Just use enough water to just cover the chicken, aiming for around 2.25 litres/quarts. Much more than this and the broth will be too weak in flavour. Chicken size: This recipe will work fine for any chicken larger than 1.5kg/3lb. Any smaller and the broth flavour may be slightly lacking but you can just top up flavour with Vegeta, or chicken or vegetable stock powder/crumbled bouillon cube. Will also work with: drumsticks, bone in skin on thighs. Noodles: Any pasta or noodles work perfectly here. Short, wide twisted noodles/pasta are ideal for ease of eating. Break long strand pasta into 3 or 4. Vegeta – Vegeta is a brand of vegetable stock powder, labelled "Gourmet Seasoning" and "All Purpose Seasoning" in the US & Canada. You can use stock powder (same amount) or crumbled bouillon cube (1 for each teaspoon) – use chicken or vegetable. Cooked carrots & celery – I tend to discard because stove simmering for this long makes them extremely soft, bordering on mushy. If using slow cooker, they tend to have a better texture so I chop and toss them in. But you can add them if you want – extra veg! Stirring lots = activates pasta starch = cloudy broth. How to store Chicken Noodle Soup: Noodles bloat! So best to separate the noodles and veggies from the soup, then store separately. Will keep for 3 – 4 days in the fridge. Broth will freeze for months.

ingredients.original_text: 1.8 kg / 3.5 lb whole chicken , rinsed and cleaned, the best quality you can afford (Note 1)
1 head of garlic , cut in half horiztonally
2 onions , peeled and cut into quarters
4 thyme sprigs (or 1 1/2 tsp dried thyme leaves)
9 – 10 cups water (enough to just cover chicken)
2 medium carrots , cut in quarters lengthwise then chopped
2 celery ribs , cut into 1 cm/ 1/2″ thick pieces
200 g/ 6 oz egg noodles or pasta of choice (Note 3)
2 – 3 tsp Vegeta or other stock powder (Note 4)

steps.instruction: Place the chicken in a large pot. Add remaining Broth ingredients, then add enough water so the chicken is just covered (too much water = weak broth).

Cover with lid, bring the water to a gentle simmer over medium heat. Then adjust heat so it's simmering GENTLY with the lid on. Simmer 1.5 hours until chicken is tender and easily comes off the bone. (Or 8 hours on low in a slow cooker)
Scoop scum off surface once or twice during first 30 minutes.
Transfer chicken to pan. Shred meat and discard bones. You'll only need half the chicken meat, save the rest for another purpose.
Remove carrots, celery etc from broth, and discard. Optional: Chop carrot and celery, add back into soup with chicken. (Note 5)
Optional: Refrigerate soup for a few hours until the fat sets, then scoop the fat off the surface (I do this, healthier & makes soup clearer).
Heat oil in a large pot over medium meat. Add onion and garlic, cook for 2 minutes. Add celery and carrots, cook for 5 minutes until softened and a bit sweet, don't let them brown.
Turn heat up to medium high. Gently pour 8 cups broth in (top up with water if necessary), leaving behind residue settled in the bottom of the broth pot.
Add Vegeta (stock powder), thyme, bay leaf and pepper. Stir.
When it starts bubbling, add noodles/pasta. Cook for time per packet MINUS 2 minutes. Stir gently only once or twice (Note 6).
Add half the chicken, stir (it warms quickly), then season with salt and pepper to taste – season well!
Serve soup immediately, garnished with fresh parsley.

title: One-pot Moussaka beef rice pilaf
description: A cross between moussaka and risotto - eggplant and beef mince cooked in a red-wine tomato sauce with garlic and oregano, combined with rice for a complete one-pot meal.

ingredients.original_text: 500g / 1 lb beef mince / ground beef (or lamb) – Note 1
1/2 tsp cooking salt / kosher salt (for the beef)

ingredients.item: pinot noir or other dry red wine
ingredients.original_text: 1/3 cup pinot noir or other dry red wine (optional)
400g / 14 oz can chickpeas , drained (or other beans of choice)
1 1/4 tsp cooking salt / kosher salt (for broth)
Extra virgin olive oil , for drizzling
Dollop of Greek yogurt optional (not pictured but goes really well!)

steps.instruction: Cut the eggplant into 1.5cm / 3/5" cubes. I don't peel eggplant because I haven't encountered bitter skin or flesh issues (largely bred out of modern eggplants). If you are concerned, sprinkle the eggplant with 1/4 teaspoon salt and leave in a colander for 30 minutes to sweat bitterness out. Pat off the excess water then proceed with the recipe.

Heat the oil on a large pot over high heat. Add the onion and garlic, cook for 2 minutes. Add the beef and cook, breaking it up as you go, until you no longer see red. Add the 1/2 tsp salt and oregano. Cook for 1 minute. Add the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute (don't shortcut this step – Note 3).
Add the eggplant and the cubes are coated in the red beef juices. Add the wine, bring it to a simmer, stir (scrape the base of the pot) then let it mostly evaporate.

Add the rice and stir to coat. Add remaining ingredients, stir, bring to a simmer. Give it one last stir then once there are gentle bubbles all across the surface (not just around the edge), put the lid on and turn the heat down to medium low (or low, if using a strong stove).
Cook for 20 minutes, no peeking, no stirring! The liquid will be absorbed by the stage though there will be a layer of tomato on the surface (peek quickly!).
Remove the pot from the stove and rest for 10 minutes (lid still on) – residual liquid will be absorbed and the rice will finish cooking.
Toss the rice – it will be juicy but not mushy! Divide between bowls, drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle with parsley and enjoy!

title: Chicken Cacciatore (Italian chicken stew)
description: Chicken Cacciatore, 'Hunter's Stew' or Pollo Cacciatore in Italian, is a hearty, rustic stew made with chicken and vegetables simmered in a tomato sauce rich with wine and herbs.

ingredients.original_text: 4 bone in chicken thighs large (1 kg / 2 lb)
2 rosemary sprigs (about 15 cm/6″ long), or 1 tsp dried rosemary
2 bay leaves , preferably fresh else dried
3 anchovy fillets (or 1 tsp anchovy paste), optional (Note 2)
2 red capsicum , sliced 8 mm thick (medium, not giant)

ingredients.item: pinot noir or other dry red wine
ingredients.original_text: 3/4 cup pinot noir or other dry red wine (Note 3)

steps.instruction: Spread the chicken out on a tray. Sprinkle both sides with the salt and pepper.

Heat the oil over high heat in a large, deep, heavy based pan, preferably one with a lid (Note 4). Place the chicken thighs in the pan, skin side down, and cook until the skin is golden brown, around 6 minutes. Turn and cook the flesh side for just 1 minute, then remove the chicken onto the same tray. Then add the drumsticks and brown each side as best you can, about 1 1/2 minutes on each side (the shape makes it awkward). Remove onto the tray.

Pour off and discard all but about 2 tablespoons of fat. Turn the heat down to medium and let the pan cool slightly. Add the onion, rosemary leaves, bay leaves and dried oregano. Cook for 3 minutes until the onion is starting to soften.
Clear a space in the middle of the pan. Add the anchovies and garlic, cooking, mashing up the anchovies, until the garlic is light golden, then stir it into the onion.

Turn the heat back up to high. Add the mushroom and capsicum. Stir until softened – about 5 minutes (the mushrooms will go watery then the water will evaporate). Add the tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes to cook out the sour flavour – do not shortcut this.
Add the wine. Stir, bring to simmer then allow to reduce by around 75%. Add the stock, canned tomato, salt and pepper. Stir, bring to a simmer.

Then carefully place the chicken into the sauce (skin side up) and pour any juices on the tray in as well. When the liquid returns to a simmer, cover, reduce the heat to medium then simmer energetically for 20 minutes. Remove lid, add olives, simmer for a further 10 minutes (no lid). This will reduce and thicken the sauce.
Serve the chicken with plenty of sauce over mashed potato or polenta, sprinkled with parsley if desired.

title: Salisbury steak meatballs with mushroom gravy
description: Tender, savoury meatballs made with ground beef flavoured with garlic, Worcestershire, ketchup, mustard and bouillon cubes, served in a mushroom gravy.

ingredients.original_text: ▢ 3 tsp dijon mustard OR 2 tsp dry mustard powder

steps.instruction: Place the breadcrumbs in a large bowl. Using a standard box grater, grate the onion over the panko so it soaks in. Mix through.
Add remaining Meatball ingredients and mix well with your hands until thoroughly combined.
Roll 20 to 22 balls (1 1/2 tablespoons of mixture). I use an ice cream scoop with lever, handy!
Mix ingredients in a bowl until smooth. We will use this to thicken the gravy (no lumpy gravy, yay!)
Heat the oil in a large skillet over high heat. Cook meatballs, using tablespoons to roll (to minimise deforming) until browned on the outside but still raw inside. Remove onto a plate and set aside.

Melt butter in the same skillet still on high. Cook mushrooms until they start to soften (~ 2 minutes). Add salt, pepper and the garlic, then cook for a further 2 minutes until the garlic is golden and mushrooms are soft.
Add beef stock, salt, pepper and slurry, then stir to combine.

Once the liquid starts to simmer, add meatballs and any juices pooled on the plate. Adjust heat to medium so it's simmering gently and cook for 5 minutes until gravy is thickened and meatballs are cooked through.
Serve over mash sprinkled with parsley if desired.

title: Chinese Dumplings – Pork (Potstickers)
description: Pan fried Chinese dumplings with a traditional pork and cabbage filling, golden crispy on the underside and steamy on top.

ingredients.original_text: 1 ½ cups finely chopped Chinese cabbage (Napa cabbage)
250 g / 0.5 lb fatty pork mince (20 – 30% fat ideal – Note 2)
¼ cup garlic chives , finely chopped*
2 tsp light soy sauce (light or dark soy also ok)
¼ tsp white pepper (black also ok)

steps.instruction: Place the mushrooms in a bowl and pour over plenty of boiled water. Leave for 20 minutes or until rehydrated. Squeeze out excess water, then finely chop.
Place cabbage in a bowl with salt. Toss with fingers, then set aside for 15 minutes. Squeeze out excess liquid from cabbage using hands.
Place cabbage, mushrooms and remaining Filling ingredients in a bowl. Mix with your hands until well combined.
Peel one wrapper off and place on the palm of your left hand (if right handed). Dip your finger in water and run it along half the edge of the wrapper.
Place 1 heaped tablespoon of Filling in the centre. Fold wrapper over, then pleat to seal. Alternatively, just press together with no pleats.
Finish so the dumpling is curved slightly, see photos in post, with the pleats on the top.
Place on tray. Cover with cling wrap or wet tea towel (important). Repeat with remaining dumplings. Should make 30, if yours are extremely plump you may only make 25.
Make sure your pan has a lid that fits it half decently (Note 6).
Heat 2 tsp oil in a non stick pan over medium high heat. Add dumplings, pressing down firmly to flatten the base onto the pan. Cook around 8 to 10 per batch.
When the base is golden brown (check them), add 1/2 cup of water into the pan.
Immediately clamp the lid on, then leave for 7 minutes (any less and the pleats won't be cooked through so if your water dries out, add a bit more).
Remove lid – most of the water should be evaporated, the pleats should be cooked through. Leave the pan on the stove until the base dries and the underside of the dumplings are once again crisp.
Remove dumplings from pan and transfer to serving plate. Repeat with remaining Dumplings.
Serve with Dipping Sauces of choice. (Note 7)

title: Dal (Indian Lentil Curry)
description: A northern Indian dal tadka featuring yellow lentils transformed with simple spices into a flavorful, economical curry. Topped with optional sizzling spiced oil for extra depth.

ingredients.original_text: 2 tbsp / 30 g ghee , or 1 tbsp oil + 1 tbsp/15g butter (Note 1)
2 green cayenne chillies , deseeded and cut into chunks (optional) (Note 2)
1 medium onion , finely chopped (brown or yellow)
1 cup dried chana dal , yellow split peas or other yellow lentils (Note 4 for other lentils)
1 1/2 tbsp / 20g ghee , or half each butter + oil (Note 1)
1 eschalot or 1/4 small onion , halved lengthways and sliced (Note 5)
3 dried chillies , broken in half, seeds removed (optional)

steps.instruction: Soak Lentils: Rinse lentils and leave to soak in plenty of water for 1 hour. Drain in colander.
Heat ghee/oil in a heavy based saucepan over high heat. Add green chillies and fry for a minute until starting to blister.
Add onions and fry until softened.
Lower heat to medium, add garlic, ginger and curry leaves. Cook for 1 minute until garlic starts to turn golden and smells amazing.
Add tomatoes and cumin, cook until tomatoes start to break down and thicken to a paste – about 2 minutes.
Add lentils, water, tumeric and salt. Stir, bring to simmer, cover and simmer gently for 1 hour. Stir two or three times during the hour.

Remove lid and simmer gently for 30 minutes to thicken, stirring every now and then. The dal is ready when it has a consistency like porridge – some lentils should be intact but some have broken down to thicken the sauce.
Stir through garam masala at the end. Adjust salt if desired.
Pour over Tadka, if using, and stir through.
Serve Dal over rice, garnished with a sprig of coriander if desired.
Heat ghee in a small pan over medium heat until hot but not smoking.
Add cumin and mustard seeds, stir until cumin is slightly golden.
Then add chillies and cook for 20 seconds, then add eschallots and cook until tinged with gold. Don't let the spices burn!
Immediately pour into Dahl.

title: Lamb Korma curry
description: Rich with coconut cream and cashews, Lamb Korma is gently spiced and mildly sweet. Fall-apart tender lamb in a creamy, family-friendly sauce.

ingredients.original_text: 2 large onions , cut into 1cm/1/2″ squares (brown or yellow)
800g / 1.6 lb lamb shoulder (Note 2), trimmed off excess fat, cut into 4-5 cm / 2" cubes
1 tsp kashmiri chilli powder (or smoked paprika + cayenne – Note 4)
10 cardamom pods (green), lightly crushed, wrapped and tied in muslin or cheesecloth (Note 5)
1/2 cup cream , full-fat (any type – thickened/heavy, pure etc)(Note 6)

steps.instruction: Place the onion, garlic and ginger into a tall container that fits the head of a stick blender. Blitz until smooth – about 5 to 10 seconds. Scrape into a bowl, set aside.
In the same jar, while covering the mouth of the jar with your hand (to prevent flying cashew comets), blitz the cashews and water with the stick blender until smooth, about 10 seconds. Set aside.
Preheat the oven to 180°C/350°F (160°C fan-forced).
Melt and heat ghee in a ~24 cm/ 10" oven-proof pot over high-heat. Cook the onion puree, stirring regularly, for 8 minutes until it has reduced by half. Don't let it caramelise.
Add lamb, reduce to medium-high heat then stir until the meat changes from red to light brown. Don't try to brown the meat (it won't happen).

Add turmeric, ground ginger, garam marsala and kashmiri chilli, then stir through. Pour in the stock (the liquid should just barely cover the meat). Add cardamom pod sachet and cinnamon sticks, then stir. Bring to a simmer, cover with a lid then transfer to the oven.

Cook for 1 1/2 hours. Stir in Cashew Puree, put the lid back on then return to the oven for another 30 minutes. The sauce should have thickened to a creamy consistency and lamb should be tender end to pull apart with forks with no effort.
Place the pot on a low heat. Add the sugar, salt, coconut cream and cream, then stir. Simmer for 2 minutes, then you're done!
Serve over basmati rice with a sprinkle of cashew nuts and coriander, if desired. Naan for dunking highly recommended!

title: Rosemary Garlic Grilled Lamb Chops
description: Grilled lamb chops infused with rosemary garlic flavours! The marinade is made with red wine vinegar which pairs well with lamb because it cuts through the richness of the strong lamb flavour, and it tenderises economical cuts of lamb.

ingredients.original_text: 600 – 800 g / 1.2 – 1.6lb lamb chops (4 – 8, depending on size)
1 1/4 tsp finely chopped fresh rosemary (or 1 tsp dried rosemary)

steps.instruction: Mix Marinade ingredients in a bowl until salt dissolves. Add lamb, mix to coat, then marinate at least 2 hours, preferably overnight, up to 24 hours.
Brush BBQ bars with oil if required (Note 1) and heat to medium high (or heat 1/2 tbsp oil in skillet on stove on medium high).
Cook lamb to your taste. I had 4 x 150g/5oz lamb loin chops about 2cm/3/4″ thick and 3 minutes on each side = medium. I also cooked smashed garlic cloves and rosemary sprigs for garnish.
Transfer lamb to plate, cover loosely with foil and rest for 5 minutes.
Serve with sides of choice!

title: Sticky Grilled Chicken
description: Sticky, sweet and savoury grilled chicken marinated to tenderise and infuse with flavour, finished with a shiny glaze.

ingredients.original_text: 1/4 cup soy sauce , all purpose or light (NOT dark)

steps.instruction: Mix Marinade in a large bowl.
Scoop out 1/2 cup and reserve for basting ("Glaze").
Add chicken, turn to coat. Marinade overnight (minimum 3 hours, up to 48 hours, or freeze).
Brush BBQ with vegetable or canola oil, then preheat to medium.
Pick up chicken, allowing excess Marinade to drip off.
Place on BBQ, cook 4 minutes until nicely caramelised.
Turn chicken, then brush generously with Glaze. Cook 4 minutes.
Flip, brush with more Glaze, flip again and brush with Glaze.
Remove chicken onto plate, cover loosely with foil.
Rest 3 minutes before serving, sprinkled with parsley if using.
Stove – follow up steps in a non stick pan, heated to medium high with 1 tbsp oil (cook in batches if needed).
Oven – use this recipe: Sticky Baked Chicken

title: Thai red curry pot roast chicken
description: A spectacular one-pot dish featuring a whole chicken cooked in Thai red curry sauce with potatoes and beans. The pot-roasting method keeps the chicken ultra juicy while the sauce develops incredible depth from store-bought curry paste.

ingredients.original_text: 115g/ 4 oz (1/2 cup) Thai red curry paste
6 kaffir lime leaves, crushed in hand

steps.instruction: Remove chicken from refrigerator 1 hour before cooking. Pat dry and sprinkle with salt. Preheat oven to 200°C/400°F (180°C fan).
Heat oil in large oven-proof pot with lid over medium-high heat. Cook curry paste, garlic, ginger and lemongrass for 2 minutes.
Add chicken stock, stir, and simmer rapidly for 3 minutes to reduce by half. Stir in coconut cream, crushed kaffir lime leaves, sugar and fish sauce.
Place chicken into sauce and spoon sauce over it. Surround with potatoes. Cover with lid and bake for 40 minutes.
Remove lid and baste chicken. Increase oven to 220°C/425°F (200°C fan). Bake uncovered for 10 minutes, baste, then bake another 10 minutes.
Push green beans into sauce. Baste again and bake for final 10 minutes uncovered.
Remove chicken to a plate and rest for 10 minutes. Cover pot to keep sauce warm.
Carve chicken and place on platter. Stir basil leaves into sauce. Pour sauce, potatoes and beans over chicken. Serve with jasmine rice.

title: Vietnamese Chicken Salad
description: A slaw-like Vietnamese chicken salad bursting with fresh herbs and bright flavors. Features shredded vegetables, tender chicken, and a zingy Nuoc Cham dressing.

ingredients.original_text: 350g/12oz cooked chicken, cut into thin batons
6 heaped cups wombok cabbage (Napa cabbage), finely shredded
1 red capsicum / bell pepper, finely sliced
1/2 cup peanuts, roasted unsalted, finely chopped

steps.instruction: Shake dressing ingredients in a jar. Set aside 10 minutes to let flavours meld.
Place all salad ingredients in a very large bowl. Pour over half the dressing and toss well. Set aside 5 minutes.
Just before serving, toss again then add most of the remaining dressing. Taste then add more dressing if desired.
Serve sprinkled with lots of peanuts.

title: Mexican Corn Salad
description: A fresh corn salad inspired by esquites, a Mexican street snack. Corn kernels are sautéed in butter and garlic, then dressed with a creamy lime dressing, cilantro, and jalapeño.

ingredients.original_text: 5 cups corn cut from the cob (~4 large ears)
1 tbsp Jalapeno, deseeded and finely chopped
1 cup coriander / cilantro leaves, roughly chopped

steps.instruction: Cut corn kernels from the cob, or use frozen corn without thawing.
Melt butter in large skillet over high heat. Add garlic and stir for 10 seconds. Add corn and cook for 5 minutes until golden brown.
Transfer corn to large bowl. Add mayonnaise, sour cream, lime juice, and parmesan. Toss well.
Add cilantro, red onion, green onion, and jalapeño. Toss again.
Top with crumbled cotija or feta. Serve warm or at room temperature.

title: Thai Basil Chicken
description: An incredibly fast and easy Thai chicken stir fry that tastes just like restaurant quality, served over jasmine rice. Spicy, garlicky and savoury with a hint of sweet, ready in just 15 minutes.

ingredients.original_text: 225g / 7oz chicken thigh fillets, skinless boneless, bite size
1 cup Thai basil leaves, loosely packed
1 birds eye or Thai chilli, deseeded and finely chopped

steps.instruction: Mix sauce ingredients together in a small bowl.
Heat oil in wok over high heat.
Add garlic and chilli, cook for 10 seconds.
Add white part of green onions and chicken, fry until cooked, around 2 minutes.
Add sauce and cook for 1 minute until thick glossy sauce forms.
Toss through green part of green onions and basil leaves. Stir until wilted, then serve with jasmine rice.

title: Thai Green Curry
description: Quick 30-minute Thai green curry made with store-bought curry paste, freshened with garlic, ginger, and lemongrass. Features chicken, eggplant, and snow peas in a creamy coconut sauce.

ingredients.original_text: 350 g/12 oz chicken thigh, skinless boneless, sliced

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a pot over medium high heat.
Add curry paste and cook for 2 to 3 minutes until mostly dried out.
Add chicken broth and coconut milk, mix to dissolve paste.
Add fish sauce, sugar, kaffir lime leaves. Bring to simmer.
Add chicken, cook 7 minutes on medium.
Add eggplants, cook 5 minutes until soft.
Add snow peas, cook 2 minutes. Stir through basil and lime juice.
Serve over jasmine rice.

title: The most amazing canned tuna pasta
description: A quick 15-minute pantry pasta featuring canned tuna in oil, anchovies, capers, and lemon. This economical dish proves canned tuna can be delicious with the right flavor combinations.

steps.instruction: Cook pasta in salted boiling water per packet minus 1 minute. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
Heat reserved oil from tuna cans in same pot over medium heat.
Add garlic, anchovies and chilli flakes. Cook and stir for 1 minute.
Add cooked pasta, capers and 1/3 cup pasta water. Toss for 2 minutes until sauce thickens.
Turn off heat. Add tuna, parsley, lemon zest, juice, salt and pepper. Toss gently. Serve immediately.

title: Char Siu Pork (Chinese BBQ Pork)
description: Sticky, crimson red Chinese BBQ pork made at home with an easy marinade. Slow roasted until juicy inside with an incredible sticky glaze.

ingredients.original_text: 1.2–1.5kg pork scotch fillet or pork shoulder

steps.instruction: Cut pork in half to make two long strips.
Mix marinade ingredients. Marinate pork 24–48 hours in fridge.
Preheat oven to 160°C/320°F. Line tray with foil and place rack on top.
Remove pork from marinade (save marinade). Roast 30 minutes.
Simmer reserved marinade with extra honey for 2 minutes until syrupy.
Baste pork and roast 30 more minutes.
Baste, turn, brush again and roast 20 more minutes.
Baste surface and bake 10 more minutes until caramelized. Rest 10 minutes before slicing.

title: African Chicken Curry – Kuku Paka
description: A simple African-Indian coconut chicken curry with tomato and warm spices. Uses only pantry-friendly ingredients but tastes deeply flavourful.

steps.instruction: Pat chicken dry and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Heat oil over high heat. Sear thighs skin-side down 4–5 minutes, then 1 minute other side. Brown drumsticks 2 minutes per side. Transfer to plate.
Reduce heat to medium-high. Sauté onion 1 minute. Add garlic and ginger, 30 seconds. Add spices, stir 30 seconds.
Add coconut milk, tomato and salt. Return chicken to pot. Submerge as much as possible.
Cover and simmer 10 minutes, remove lid and simmer 20 minutes more.
Stir in lemon juice and half the coriander. Serve garnished with remaining coriander over rice.

title: Easy Soft Flatbread (No Yeast)
description: A simple yeast-free flatbread that's soft and pliable, perfect for wraps, gyros, or as naan for curries. Made with just flour, butter, milk, and salt.

steps.instruction: Combine butter and milk and heat until butter is just melted.
Combine flour, salt, butter and milk in a bowl.
Knead a few minutes until smooth. Add extra flour if sticky.
Wrap with cling wrap and rest at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Cut dough into 6 pieces, roll into balls, then roll out to 20cm rounds.
Heat a non-stick pan over high heat with no oil. Cook 1–1.5 minutes until puffed, flip and cook 45 seconds.
Stack cooked bread and wrap with a tea towel to soften.

title: Naan – chewy & fluffy
description: A fluffy, bubbly, and chewy naan bread recipe that rivals restaurant quality. Made with yeast and requiring no kneading, this Indian flatbread is surprisingly easy to prepare at home.

steps.instruction: Mix yeast with warm water and sugar. Leave 10 minutes until foamy.
Whisk milk and egg. Sift flour and salt in a bowl.
Add yeast mixture, butter and egg mixture to flour. Mix until dough comes together (no kneading).
Cover and leave in a warm place 1–1.5 hours until doubled.
Cut into 6 pieces, shape into balls. Rest 15 minutes.
Roll out each ball to 3–4mm thick rounds.
Heat cast iron skillet over high heat until smoking. Cook naan 60–90 seconds until deep golden, flip and cook 45–60 seconds.
Brush with melted butter. Sprinkle with nigella seeds and coriander. Serve hot.

title: Beef Empanadas
description: Golden, flaky pastry filled with juicy beef, chorizo, potatoes, olives, and hard boiled egg. A South American handheld pie that's crispy outside and bursting with flavor inside.

steps.instruction: Cook filling: sauté onion and garlic, add chorizo and beef, cook until browned. Add tomato paste and spices.
Add stock and passata. Simmer 15 minutes until potato is tender. Stir through green onion. Cool completely.
Make pastry: pulse flour, salt and cold butter until fine crumbs. Add egg yolk and milk, blitz until dough forms.
Knead briefly, shape into 2 discs, wrap and refrigerate 30 minutes.
Roll to 3mm thick. Cut 15cm rounds. Refrigerate.
Preheat oven to 220°C. Fill each round with 3 tbsp filling, olives and egg. Fold, seal and crimp edges.
Brush with egg yolk. Bake 20 minutes until golden and crispy.

title: Very Crispy Corn Fritters
description: Golden, crispy corn fritters made with minimal batter and maximum corn flavor. Perfect for breakfast, lunch, or dinner.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 120°C. Set wire rack on baking tray.
Mix flour, cornflour and baking soda. Add egg and milk, mix until incorporated.
Add corn, parmesan, green onions and coriander. Mix until corn is evenly coated.
Heat oil in skillet over medium-high. Scoop 1/4 cup batter, flatten to 1cm thick. Cook 3 minutes per side until deep golden.
Keep warm in oven. Repeat with remaining batter. Serve with avocado sauce or sour cream.

title: Rogan Josh
description: An Indian lamb curry featuring fall-apart tender meat in a creamy tomato sauce with intense spices. All ingredients are readily available at standard grocery stores.

steps.instruction: Melt ghee over medium heat. Add cinnamon, cardamom and cloves, cook 1 minute.
Add onion and cook 7 minutes until golden. Add garlic and ginger, cook 1 minute.
Stir in all spices, cook 30 seconds.
Mix in tomato passata and salt, add stock. Add lamb and stir, bring to simmer.
Cover and simmer on low for 1 hour 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove lid and cook 15 minutes to reduce sauce.
Stir in yogurt, extra garam masala and fennel. Cook a few more minutes.
Serve with basmati rice, garnished with fresh coriander.

title: Pie Crust (Shortcrust Pastry)
description: All-butter flaky pie crust made with classic shortcrust pastry that comes together in 1 minute using a food processor. Perfect for both sweet and savory pies.

ingredients.original_text: 115g unsalted butter, cold, cut into cubes

steps.instruction: Pulse flour, sugar and salt in food processor.
Scatter cold butter cubes and pulse 5 times until largest pieces resemble chickpeas.
With motor running, drizzle in 2.5 tbsp ice cold water.
Blitz 10 seconds until crumbly dough forms.
Tip onto work surface and bring together into a dome. Pat into disc, wrap and refrigerate 1 hour.
Roll out on floured surface into circle 10cm larger than pie dish. Transfer to tin.
Blind bake at 200°C for 15 minutes with weights, then 5 minutes uncovered.

title: Family Meat Pie
description: An Australian-style family-size meat pie featuring slow-cooked beef in rich Guinness gravy with a shortcrust base and golden puff pastry lid.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 180°C. Season and brown beef aggressively in batches. Remove.
Cook bacon until golden, remove. Melt butter and cook mushrooms. Remove.
Cook onion, garlic, carrot, celery 5 minutes. Add beef, bacon, Guinness, stock and water.
Cover and bake 1 hour. Add mushrooms, bake another hour.
Simmer on stovetop 20 minutes uncovered. Mix butter and flour into beurre manié, stir into sauce.
Cool filling completely. Blind bake shortcrust in 23cm pie dish.
Fill crust with filling. Top with puff pastry, crimp edges, brush with egg wash.
Bake at 200°C for 45-50 minutes until golden. Serve with ketchup.

title: Ultra Lazy Healthy Chicken and Broccoli Pasta Bake
description: A complete one-dish meal with minimal prep featuring chicken, broccoli, and pasta in a creamy sauce made without cream. Ready in 45 minutes with just 10 minutes of prep.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 200°C.
Season chicken with salt, pepper and thyme.
Spread pasta in baking dish. Sprinkle flour. Pour over milk and broth, add garlic and butter. Stir.
Spread chicken over top, then broccoli.
Cover with foil, bake 15 minutes. Remove foil, stir, add most cheese, stir. Top with remaining cheese.
Bake 15–20 minutes until golden. Stand a few minutes before serving.

title: EMERGENCY Corn Chowder Soup
description: A quick and effortless hearty soup requiring minimal prep, using canned corn and pantry staples. Ready in 15 minutes total.

steps.instruction: Cook bacon in pot over medium high heat until golden. Remove and chop.
Add garlic and stir 20 seconds. Add creamed corn, kernels, stock, milk, potato, salt and pepper.
Simmer 5 minutes until potato is tender.
Mix milk and cornflour, pour into soup. Stir and simmer 1 minute until thickened.
Ladle into bowls, top with green onion and bacon.

title: Éclairs
description: Light-as-air choux pastry éclairs filled with stabilised Chantilly cream and topped with shiny chocolate glaze. A foolproof recipe that rivals even the finest patisseries.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 180°C. Melt butter with water until simmering.
Add flour and salt. Stir vigorously until dough forms and leaves pan sides. Cool 10 minutes.
Add eggs one at a time, mixing until fully incorporated.
Pipe 12–14cm logs onto lined trays. Bake 45 minutes, opening door briefly at 30, 35 and 40 mins.
Cool completely. Slice each éclair horizontally.
Heat cream with corn syrup until just below boiling. Pour over chocolate, stand 1 minute, stir smooth. Dip lids. Refrigerate 30 minutes.
Whip cream, mascarpone, icing sugar and vanilla to stiff peaks. Pipe onto bases. Top with glazed lids.

title: Chicken with Creamy Sun Dried Tomato Sauce
description: Tender chicken in a ridiculously addictive creamy sun dried tomato sauce, ready in just 20 minutes. A quick weeknight winner.

ingredients.original_text: 750g chicken thighs or breast, skinless boneless
1/2 cup sun dried tomatoes, strips, drained

ingredients.item: oil from sun dried tomatoes jar
ingredients.original_text: 2 tbsp oil from sun dried tomatoes jar

steps.instruction: Season chicken with salt and pepper.
Heat sun dried tomato oil in skillet over high heat. Cook chicken until golden on both sides. Remove.
Add garlic, cook 15 seconds.
Add wine, simmer 1 minute scraping up browned bits.
Mix in mustard, then add broth, cream, parmesan and sun dried tomatoes. Simmer 2–3 minutes until thickened.
Stir through basil, return chicken, simmer 1 minute. Serve immediately.

title: Oven Baked Chicken and Rice
description: A one-pan dinner featuring buttery garlic rice and juicy seasoned baked chicken thighs, cooked together in the oven with minimal prep time.

ingredients.original_text: 5 bone in chicken thigh fillets, skin removed

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Scatter onion and garlic in baking dish with butter in centre. Bake 15 minutes.
Mix together chicken rub ingredients. Sprinkle on both sides of chicken.
Add rice to dish and mix. Place chicken on rice. Pour broth and water around chicken.
Cover with foil, bake 30 minutes. Remove foil, bake 20 more minutes until liquid absorbed.
Stand 5 minutes, fluff rice and serve.

title: Quiche Lorraine
description: A classic French quiche with a buttery shortcrust pastry filled with bacon, eggs, cream, and cheese. Elegant and surprisingly easy to make.

steps.instruction: Prepare and blind bake shortcrust pastry in 23cm tin. Preheat oven to 200°C.
Cook onion, garlic and bacon in butter until bacon is golden. Transfer to paper towel, cool.
Whisk eggs, cream, salt and pepper.
Scatter bacon mixture in crust. Scatter cheese. Pour egg mixture over top.
Bake 35–40 minutes until golden. Rest 10 minutes before serving.

title: Slow Cooked Lamb Shanks in Red Wine Sauce
description: Lamb shanks braised until meltingly tender in a rich, deeply flavored red wine sauce. A restaurant-quality dish perfect for winter entertaining.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 180°C. Season shanks with salt and pepper.
Sear shanks in batches in hot oil until browned. Remove.
Cook onion and garlic 2 minutes. Add carrot and celery, cook 5 minutes.
Add red wine, stock, tomatoes, tomato paste, thyme and bay leaves. Return shanks.
Bring to simmer, cover, bake 2 hours. Remove lid, bake 30 more minutes.
Remove shanks. Strain sauce, reduce if needed to syrupy consistency.
Serve shanks over mashed potato with sauce.

title: Garlic Butter Pan Fried Chicken Breasts
description: Pan-fried chicken breasts with a crisp exterior and tender juicy interior, finished with a garlic butter herb sauce. A quick and versatile weeknight protein.

ingredients.original_text: 1/3 cup dry white wine or chicken broth

steps.instruction: Pound chicken breasts to even thickness. Season with salt, pepper and spice rub.
Heat oil in skillet over medium-high. Add chicken and cook 6 minutes without moving.
Flip, add 1 tbsp butter, cook 5 minutes until cooked through. Remove.
Reduce heat to medium. Add 2 tbsp butter and garlic, stir until fragrant.
Add herbs, cook 10 seconds. Add wine/broth and scrape up browned bits.
Add remaining butter. Return chicken and heat 1–2 minutes. Spoon sauce over chicken.

title: Beef & Lentil Soup
description: A hearty one-pot soup combining ground beef with lentils and vegetables, flavored with Moroccan-inspired spices. Healthy, economical, and perfect for meal prep or freezing.

steps.instruction: Heat oil in large pot. Cook onion and garlic 3 minutes until golden.
Add beef, cook until no longer red. Add 2 tbsp spice mix, cook 2 minutes.
Add carrot and celery, stir 1 minute.
Add stock, tomato, lentils and remaining spice mix. Simmer covered 15 minutes.
Add zucchini and green beans. Simmer 10 more minutes.
Serve with yogurt and coriander.

title: Mediterranean Chickpea Egg Salad
description: Simple and satisfying chickpea egg salad Mediterranean-style with fresh vegetables, herbs, and a zesty Dijon-lemon dressing. Skips mayonnaise for a lighter dish.

steps.instruction: Mix dressing ingredients in a small bowl or mason jar.
Add all salad ingredients except eggs to large bowl. Pour dressing and toss.
Add sliced eggs and mix gently. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Set aside a few minutes before serving.

title: Slow Cooker Shredded Beef Chili
description: A signature chili recipe featuring pulled beef slow-cooked in a rich, heavily spiced sauce until tender. Offers deeper flavors than traditional ground beef chili.

steps.instruction: Season and brown beef in batches in hot oil. Remove.
Sauté garlic, onion and capsicum until caramelized. Deglaze with broth. Transfer to slow cooker.
Add beans, tomato, tomato paste, broth and chili spices. Stir. Add beef. Cook on low 8 hours.
Remove beef and shred. Mix cornflour with broth, stir into slow cooker. Add shredded beef, cook 20 more minutes.
Serve with sour cream, cheese, coriander, avocado and corn chips.

title: French Lentil Ragout
description: A classic French way of cooking lentils where aromatics are sautéed before simmering Puy lentils in stock. An outstanding side dish that pairs perfectly with any protein.

steps.instruction: Heat oil in saucepan. Sauté garlic, carrot and onion 5 minutes until softened.
Add tomato paste, bay leaf and thyme. Cook 2 minutes.
Add lentils, stock, salt and pepper. Simmer uncovered 15 minutes until lentils are soft.
Stir through parsley. Season to taste.

title: Crispy Skin Fish
description: Pan-searing technique to achieve shatteringly crunchy fish skin using dry skin, hot oil, and light pressing. Works for any skin-on fish fillet.

ingredients.original_text: 180g x 2 fish fillets, skin on, pin boned

steps.instruction: Pat fish skin dry with paper towels. For thick fillets, score skin with 4cm slashes.
Season flesh and skin with salt and pepper just before cooking.
Heat oil in non-stick skillet over medium-high until smoking. Place fish skin-side down and press lightly 10 seconds.
Cook skin side 2–3 minutes until crispy.
Transfer thick fillets to 200°C oven for 10 minutes. Thin fillets: flip in pan and cook 1–2 minutes.
Rest skin-side up 3 minutes. Serve immediately.

title: Coq au Vin
description: A French classic featuring bone-in chicken braised in red wine with bacon, mushrooms, and onions, creating a rich, glossy sauce that's luxurious and deeply flavorful.

steps.instruction: Marinate chicken with onions, bay, thyme and wine overnight (12–24 hours).
Strain wine, reduce by half over medium-high heat. Set aside.
Pat chicken dry, season. Sear in batches until browned. Remove.
Cook bacon until golden. Sauté mushrooms until golden. Cook onions until golden patches.
Add butter, garlic, tomato paste and flour. Cook 2 minutes each. Stir in stock and reduced wine.
Add chicken, bacon, mushrooms, herbs. Bring to simmer.
Cover and bake at 180°C for 45 minutes. Serve over mashed potato.

title: Pork Chop Marinade
description: A simple marinade that makes pork chops exceptionally juicy with savory flavor and caramelized crust. Even just one hour of marinating creates remarkable results.

steps.instruction: Mix marinade in a ziplock bag. Add chops and massage to coat. Marinate 1–24 hours.
Take chops out of fridge 20 minutes before cooking.
Cook on BBQ or pan over medium-high heat 4 minutes per side until caramelized.
Baste with remaining marinade, cook basted side 30 seconds.
Rest 3 minutes before serving.

title: Homemade Vegetable Stock
description: An easy, classic vegetable stock recipe far superior to store-bought. Essential for every kitchen and perfect for vegetarians and vegans.

steps.instruction: Place all ingredients in a large pot. Bring to simmer, reduce to low.
Simmer 2 hours without lid until reduced by about half.
Strain, pressing juices out of vegetables. You should have about 1 litre.
Use immediately or freeze in 1 cup portions.

title: Chop Suey (Chicken Stir Fry)
description: A saucy chicken stir fry loaded with tender chicken, vegetables and Chinese brown sauce. This quick 15-minute recipe uses two restaurant secrets for authentic takeout-quality results.

steps.instruction: Toss chicken with baking soda, rest 20–30 minutes. Rinse well and pat dry.
Mix cornflour and soy sauce until lump-free. Add remaining sauce ingredients.
Heat oil in wok over high heat. Add garlic, then onion. Cook 1 minute.
Add chicken, cook 1 minute until surface turns white.
Add choy sum stems, carrot and mushrooms. Stir fry 1 minute.
Add choy sum leaves, bean sprouts and sauce. Stir fry 1–2 minutes until sauce thickens. Serve with rice.

title: Smoked Sausage and Rice – Quick One Pot Meal
description: A quick one pot dinner using smoked sausage that adds tons of flavor to the rice. Tastes like jambalaya but is much faster to make.

ingredients.original_text: 1 1/2 cups long grain white rice, uncooked

steps.instruction: Cook sausages in oil until golden. Remove.
Cook garlic and onion 2 minutes. Add capsicum and cook 2 minutes.
Add rice, stock, paprika, salt, pepper and sausage. Bring to boil, reduce to low simmer.
Cover and cook 20 minutes.
Remove from stove. Add peas, replace lid, rest 10 minutes.
Fluff rice with fork. Garnish with parsley and serve.

title: Garlic Chicken Thighs
description: A quick 5-ingredient boneless chicken thigh recipe with crispy garlic crust and garlic butter sauce. Ready in just 15 minutes.

steps.instruction: Sprinkle chicken with garlic powder, salt and pepper.
Heat oil in skillet over medium. Cook chicken 5 minutes until deep golden. Flip.
Add garlic and butter, cook 1 minute until garlic light golden.
Add wine, turn up heat, simmer 1.5 minutes. Serve chicken with sauce.

title: Slow Cooker Beef Stroganoff
description: An incredibly tender slow-cooked beef smothered in creamy Stroganoff sauce with garlic mushrooms. Made with economical stewing beef for restaurant-quality flavor.

steps.instruction: Brown beef in batches in hot oil. Remove.
Cook garlic and onion 3 minutes. Sprinkle flour, stir. Add mustard and half stock, mix until smooth. Add remaining stock.
Transfer to slow cooker with beef. Cook 8 hours on LOW.
Cook mushrooms in butter until golden. Add garlic, season.
Mix sour cream with 1.5 cups liquid from slow cooker. Stir into stew. Add mushrooms. Serve over noodles or mashed potato.

title: Vindaloo
description: A traditional Indian curry from Goa featuring tender beef slow-cooked in a fiery red sauce with bold curry flavors. This spicy dish is not for the faint-hearted.

steps.instruction: Blend curry paste ingredients with water until smooth. Marinate beef 2 hours minimum.
Preheat oven to 190°C. Cook onion, ginger, garlic in ghee until translucent.
Add mustard seeds, tomato paste. Stir.
Add beef stock, puree sauce smooth. Return to pot.
Add beef with marinade and curry leaves. Bring to boil.
Cover and bake 2 hours until beef is tender.
Serve over basmati rice with naan and yogurt.

title: Taco Soup
description: A quick and easy hearty soup combining taco flavors with ground beef, corn, and beans. Everything you love about tacos served in soup form.

steps.instruction: Cook garlic and onion in oil 3 minutes.
Add beef, cook until browned.
Add all remaining ingredients and taco seasoning. Simmer 10 minutes.
Serve with corn chips, sour cream, cheese and coriander.

title: Mexican Chipotle Pork & Beans
description: A big pot of saucy, shreddable pork shoulder slow cooked in a rich chipotle sauce with creamy butter beans.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 180°C. Puree chipotle in adobo.
Season pork with salt, cumin. Brown in oil, remove.
Sauté onion and garlic. Add tomato paste and chipotle puree, cook 2 minutes each.
Add remaining ingredients except beans. Add pork. Cover and bake 2 hours.
Add beans, bake 30 minutes uncovered. Shred pork, serve with tortillas or rice.

title: Chili Lime Baked Salmon with Potatoes and Beans
description: A complete one-tray dinner featuring crunchy golden roast potatoes, salmon glazed with chili, lime and honey, and green beans. Quick to prepare with big flavors.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 180°C with baking tray inside.
Marinate salmon in lime, honey, soy and garlic for 20 minutes.
Toss potatoes in butter, salt. Spread on preheated tray. Bake 20 minutes.
Move potatoes aside. Place salmon skin-side down, add beans.
Drizzle marinade on salmon. Bake ~10 minutes.
Grill 2 minutes until golden. Rest 3 minutes, serve with lime.

title: Slow Roast Leg of Lamb
description: An ultra-easy method for roasting lamb leg for 4.5 hours until fall-apart tender with an incredible rosemary garlic infused gravy.

ingredients.original_text: 2.25 kg leg of lamb, bone in

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 170°C. Place garlic, onion and rosemary in roasting pan.
Season lamb and rub with olive oil. Place in pan. Pour stock and water around lamb. Cover with foil.
Slow roast 4.5 hours.
Remove foil. Increase oven to 200°C. Roast 40 minutes until well browned.
Rest lamb covered with foil. Make gravy from pan juices with flour. Strain and serve.

title: Chilli Lime Fish
description: Pan-seared white fish chunks coated in a syrupy, spicy lime sauce inspired by Thai street food. Quick to make with bold flavors.

steps.instruction: Season fish and dust with rice flour. Cook in oil 1.5–2 minutes per side until golden. Transfer.
Add sesame oil, sauté garlic, ginger and chilli 20 seconds.
Add sriracha, soy, sugar, water. Simmer 2 minutes until syrupy. Add lime juice.
Return fish, coat in sauce. Serve over rice.

title: Caesar Salad
description: A homemade Caesar salad featuring a creamy dressing with anchovy for authentic flavor. Can be served as a side or topped with chicken and egg for a complete meal.

steps.instruction: Blend all dressing ingredients until smooth. Set aside 20 minutes.
Cook bacon until golden. Cool, chop.
Toast bread, rub with garlic, cube. Bake at 180°C until golden croutons.
Toss lettuce with half the dressing.
Transfer to bowl. Top with chicken, egg, bacon and croutons. Sprinkle parmesan.

title: Chicken Fricassée (French chicken stew)
description: A traditional French chicken stew made with browned chicken pieces braised in a creamy white mushroom sauce. Quick enough for midweek dinners.

steps.instruction: Pat chicken dry with paper towels, sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Brown chicken in butter, skin-side down. Remove.
Cook mushrooms and onion 5 minutes. Add garlic and flour, cook 1 minute.
Add wine, stock, salt and pepper. Return chicken, skin-side up.
Simmer covered 10 minutes, then uncovered 20 minutes.
Remove chicken. Add cream, simmer. Return chicken. Sprinkle parsley and serve.

title: Easy Roast Potatoes
description: Simple roast potatoes using baby potatoes with garlic and thyme. Quick prep with no peeling needed, delivering golden exteriors and soft interiors.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 200°C.
Toss potatoes with oil, salt, pepper, garlic and thyme.
Spread on tray. Roast 30 minutes.
Toss, roast 25–35 more minutes until golden. Serve immediately.

title: Coleslaw
description: A creamy traditional coleslaw with homemade dressing from mayonnaise, sour cream, vinegar and mustard. This simple side gets better with time.

steps.instruction: Mix dressing ingredients in a bowl.
Add shredded cabbage and carrot, toss well.
Set aside at least 20 minutes before serving, preferably 2 hours.
Toss well before serving.

title: Slow Cooker Beef Pot Roast
description: Fall-apart beef, tender vegetables, and potatoes smothered in rich gravy. This slow cooker pot roast features a thickened sauce rather than watery broth.

steps.instruction: Season and aggressively brown beef in oil. Transfer to slow cooker.
Cook onion and garlic 2 minutes. Add wine, reduce by half. Transfer to slow cooker.
Mix flour with 1 cup broth, add to slow cooker. Add remaining broth, vegetables and herbs.
Cook on LOW 5 hours. Add potato, cook 3 more hours.
Remove beef, slice. Serve with vegetables and sauce.

title: Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry
description: A quick and easy stir fry with tender chicken and broccoli in a savory homemade sauce. Extra saucy for soaking into rice.

steps.instruction: Tenderise chicken with baking soda 20 minutes. Rinse and pat dry.
Steam broccoli until cooked. Drain well.
Mix sauce: cornflour, soy, cooking wine, oyster sauce, sesame oil, water.
Stir fry garlic and onion 10 seconds. Add chicken, cook 2 minutes.
Add broccoli and sauce. Cook 1–2 minutes until sauce thickens. Serve over rice.

title: Real Chinese All Purpose Stir Fry Sauce (Charlie!)
description: An authentic Chinese all-purpose stir fry sauce that takes minutes to make and stores in the fridge for weeks. Versatile base for stir fries and noodles.

ingredients.original_text: 1/4 cup Chinese wine or dry sherry

steps.instruction: Combine all ingredients in a jar and shake to combine. Store in refrigerator and shake before each use.

title: Magic Broccoli
description: Roasted broccoli with crispy golden edges, finished with lemon juice, zest and parmesan. A simple side dish that transforms humble broccoli into something magical.

ingredients.original_text: 2 medium-large heads of broccoli, about 1 kg

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 220°C.
Toss broccoli with oil, garlic, salt and pepper. Spread in single layer.
Roast 20–25 minutes until tips are lightly browned and crispy.
Immediately drizzle with lemon juice and zest. Toss with parmesan. Serve warm.

title: Easy Chinese Beef Stir Fry
description: A classic Chinese beef stir fry with tender velveted beef and authentic stir fry sauce. Features colorful vegetables and delivers restaurant-quality results.

steps.instruction: Coat beef with baking soda, rest 20 minutes. Rinse and pat dry.
Mix sauce. Reserve 1.5 tbsp for beef marinade, add cornstarch and water to rest.
Marinate beef in reserved sauce.
Stir fry garlic and onion 10 seconds. Add beef, cook 1.5 minutes.
Add carrots, capsicum, bok choy stems. Stir fry 1.5 minutes.
Pour in sauce, stir until thickened. Add bok choy leaves, toss 1 minute. Serve over rice.

title: Moussaka (Greek Beef and Eggplant Bake)
description: A traditional Greek dish layering soft eggplant with rich beef filling spiced with oregano and cinnamon, topped with a thick béchamel sauce. An iconic comfort food.

steps.instruction: Salt eggplant slices, rest 30 minutes. Pat dry. Brush with oil and bake at 240°C until browned. Set aside.
Cook onion and garlic in oil. Add beef, cook until browned.
Add wine, tomatoes, tomato paste, broth, oregano, cinnamon. Simmer 15 minutes until thick.
Make béchamel: cook butter and flour 1 minute, whisk in milk until thick. Add cheese, nutmeg, eggs.
Lower oven to 180°C. Layer: eggplant, meat filling, eggplant, béchamel. Sprinkle breadcrumbs.
Bake 30–40 minutes until golden. Stand 10 minutes before serving.

title: 5 Ingredient Chocolate Peanut Butter Bars (NO BAKE!)
description: Recipe VIDEO above. These no bake bars just happen to only require 5 ingredients. The hardest part is waiting for them to set!

ingredients.item: / 1 US stick  melted salted butter ((or unsalted butter + 1/4 tsp salt))
ingredients.original_text: 115g / 1/2 cup / 1 US stick  melted salted butter ((or unsalted butter + 1/4 tsp salt))

ingredients.item: crushed Arnott's Marie crackers (11 crackers) or graham cracker crumbs (8 crackers) (Note 1)
ingredients.original_text: 1 cup crushed Arnott's Marie crackers (11 crackers) or graham cracker crumbs (8 crackers) (Note 1)

ingredients.item: dark chocolate chips - the melting kind ((US: semi sweet) (Note 2))
ingredients.original_text: 1 cup dark chocolate chips - the melting kind ((US: semi sweet) (Note 2))

steps.instruction: Butter and line a 8" / 20cm square dish or pan with baking paper.
Place the Base ingredients in a bowl and mix until combined. Press into the prepared pan.
Melt the Topping ingredients (I do it in the microwave in 2 x 30 second bursts). Pour and spread over the Base - smooth the top or do swirls (see photos for examples of each).

Refrigerate until set - about 1 1/2 hours. Remove from fridge, allow to come to room temp slightly (so top cuts neatly). Cut into 16 squares (or whatever shape you want!). Best served at room temperature.

author_notes:
Recipe Notes:* Originally published June 2015, updated with new photos, words and recipe video. No change to recipe.
1. Biscuits – If you are in the UK, Digestives are great for this recipe. Otherwise, any other plain sweet crisp biscuit (i.e. not a soft biscuit with filling) should work fine – the base has peanut butter as the glue so it’s a more flexible recipe than, for example, cheesecake bases.
You could even try biscuits like oatmeal or chocolate chip biscuits! 
To make the crumbs, you can use a food processor or pop them in a ziplock bag and use a rolling pin or can to bash them. Very therapeutic!
2. Chocolate – Make sure you use chocolate for cooking/baking, not the eating kind! And if your store sells many types of chocolate chips, get the MELTING chocolate chips (as opposed to the ones that hold their shape in cookies / muffins etc) as they melt easier and smoother. This can be made with milk or dark chocolate. I use dark – not the expensive kind with a high cocoa %, just normal dark chocolate chips.
3. Storage – keeps very well, around 5 days in an airtight container. If it’s super hot where you are, fridge is best but serve at room temperature.
